frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Mohammed: Main bas practical baat kar raha hoon, aajkal zamaana theek nahi hai. Ladki ko akeli late tak bahar rehna, cab mein idhar-udhar ghoomna avoid karna chahiye, warna baad mein kuch ho jaye toh log bhi wahi poochte hain na, itni raat ko kyun nikli thi. Wife: Haan, pehle apni hifazat samajhni padti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their own responsibility and normalizes public scrutiny of victims instead of challenging male violence. It encodes protective restriction, inevitability framing ('zamaana theek nahi hai'), and post-assault questioning ('itni raat ko kyun nikli thi').
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
